signals.reasoning: Cover the live WLFI short. It is still green, but it has now given back roughly half of peak P&L, the warning is active again, and veto argues for protecting what remains rather than stretching for more.
BTC is stable, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has a mild constructive lean, but not enough to justify fresh risk under the event brake.
SOL is too mixed to justify action while veto is active.
BLAST is too thin and noisy to trust as an actionable setup under veto conditions.
USTC remains too thin and noisy to justify action.
ZEC has lost enough edge that it no longer belongs in the active trade bucket.
BANANA is too average on both edge and trade quality to justify action here.
MAVIA remains the clearest bearish fade because funding is extremely positive, crowding is high, and the market quality is still weak.
BLUR has some improving squeeze-long traits, but the event brake keeps it in watch mode rather than buy mode.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, which keeps it below the threshold for action.
